title: Tikka Halloumi Skewers
summary:
In Partnership With Aldi. #ad

Juicy chunks of halloumi marinated in creamy yoghurt and rich tikka masala paste, then cooked in the air fryer until golden and charred. Served with fluffy basmati rice and a herby, crunchy pomegranate salad, this is a proper weeknight winner made with affordable ingredients from Aldi that takes no time at all.

method.description: Cut the halloumi into 2cm chunks. In a bowl, mix ¼ of the yoghurt with the tikka masala paste, a drizzle of olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss in the halloumi to coat, then set aside to marinate.
Slice the courgette into 3cm-thick rounds. Set aside for threading onto skewers later.
Rinse the basmati rice, then cook it in a pan of salted boiling water according to the packet instructions. Drain and fluff with a fork once cooked.
Halve the cucumber lengthways and finely chop. Peel and finely slice the red onion. Slice the red chilli. Pick the mint leaves and roughly chop.
In a bowl, toss together the cucumber, onion, chilli, mint, and pomegranate seeds. Squeeze over the juice of ½ a lime and season with salt and pepper. Cut the remaining lime half into wedges.

Thread the marinated halloumi and courgette onto skewers. Air fry at 200°C for 15 mins until golden and slightly charred, turning halfway. If using mango chutney (optional), stir it through the remaining yoghurt now and season to taste. If not, just season the yoghurt with salt and pepper.
Divide the rice between plates. Top with the herby pomegranate salad and the skewers. Serve with the lime wedges and a dollop of the seasoned yoghurt.

tips: To store: Keep leftovers in airtight containers in the fridge for up to 3 days.
To reheat: Reheat skewers in the air fryer or oven until hot through.
Meal prep tip: Marinate the halloumi and prep the salad the night before to make dinner even quicker.
Halloumi → Swap for paneer or extra-firm tofu for a veggie/vegan alternative.
Tikka masala paste → Use korma or rogan josh paste for a less spicy or spicier twist.
Yoghurt → Use coconut or plant-based yoghurt for a dairy-free version.
Mint → Sub with fresh coriander or parsley if preferred.
Pomegranate seeds → Use chopped dried apricots or mango for a different sweet hit.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
